topic: In China employee termination: cost-risk matrix with legal decision map for foreign employers
primary_keyword: China employee termination cost risk matrix
related_keywords: China severance and unlawful termination risk

sources.title: Labor Contract Law of the People's Republic of China
sources.fit_note: Core legal basis for termination routes, severance framework, and unlawful termination exposure.

sources.title: Regulations for the Implementation of the Labor Contract Law of the PRC (State Council Decree No. 535)
sources.fit_note: Provides implementation-level detail that helps foreign teams map legal routes to practical steps.

sources.title: Interpretation of the Supreme People's Court on Issues Concerning the Application of Law in the Trial of Labor Dispute Cases (I)
sources.fit_note: Adds dispute-stage perspective and evidence sensitivity relevant to risk scoring.

sources.title: Social Insurance Law of the People's Republic of China
sources.fit_note: Supports offboarding compliance points tied to payroll and contribution closure.

sources.title: Employee Termination in China: A Guide for Employers
sources.fit_note: Provides foreign-employer-facing practical interpretation and implementation pitfalls.

source_replacement_notes: Replaced general tax-summary material as a top source because this run is legal-first and route-classification-first.
Kept China Briefing as the main practice layer and paired it with official law/regulation/judicial interpretation for stronger doctrinal grounding.
Dropped broad comparative commentary sources to reduce over-generalization risk and improve China-specific fit.
consensus: China termination decisions should be classified legally before payroll calculations are finalized.
Evidence quality and communication consistency are decisive risk controls.
Arbitration-first dispute dynamics make file integrity critical for foreign employers.
uncertainties: City-level enforcement style and evidentiary emphasis can vary.
Specific payment component handling may differ by local practice and case facts.
Official English translations may not fully capture latest Chinese-language nuances.
source_fit_assessment: Sufficient for a cost-risk matrix format because the set combines binding legal hierarchy with one strong foreign-reader practice explainer, while avoiding low-fit tactical or tax-dominant sources.
